full_reference: Carmona, D., Lajeunesse, M. J. & Johnson, M. T. J. (2011). Plant traits that predict resistance to herbivores. Functional Ecology 25, 358-367.
relevance: Primary seed for trait-herbivore associations; supports treating leaf economics and structural traits as separate candidates rather than one defence score.
limitations: Trait effects can vary by herbivore guild; not a universal sign rule.

full_reference: Hanley, M. E., Lamont, B. B., Fairbanks, M. M. & Rafferty, C. M. (2007). Plant structural traits and their role in anti-herbivore defence. Perspectives in Plant Ecology Evolution and Systematics 8, 157-178.
relevance: Primary seed for structural barriers including spines and trichomes and their consumer-specific effects.
limitations: Structural traits differ in effect by herbivore size and feeding mode.

full_reference: McCall, A. C. & Irwin, R. E. (2006). Florivory: the intersection of pollination and herbivory. Ecology Letters 9, 1351-1365.
relevance: Primary seed for floral traits exposed simultaneously to pollinators and antagonists; motivates a distinct floral barrier module.
limitations: Does not establish global data availability for specific floral-defence traits.

full_reference: Perez-Harguindeguy, N. et al. (2013). New handbook for standardised measurement of plant functional traits worldwide. Australian Journal of Botany 61, 167-234.
relevance: Defines measurement and unit harmonisation for leaf and plant functional traits.
limitations: Protocol reference, not evidence that a trait has a defence function.

full_reference: Kattge, J. et al. (2020). TRY plant trait database - enhanced coverage and open access. Global Change Biology 26, 119-188.
relevance: Primary trait-source seed; supports initial expectation of broad availability for common leaf economics traits.
limitations: Actual coverage must be audited on the selected network plant set and requested trait list.

full_reference: Fenster, C. B. et al. (2004). Pollination syndromes and floral specialization. Annual Review of Ecology Evolution and Systematics 35, 375-403.
relevance: Background for floral display access and reward traits as pollinator-facing functions.
limitations: Pollination syndrome must not be used as a predictor because it is circular for the network analysis.

full_reference: Leonard, A. S., Brent, J., Papaj, D. R. & Dornhaus, A. (2013). Floral nectar guide patterns discourage nectar robbing by bumble bees. PLOS ONE 8, e55909.
relevance: Example that a floral visual trait can affect both mutualist behaviour and antagonistic exploitation pathways.
limitations: One trait-system example; not a universal floral-colour effect.
